name: Dr Luke Auld-Thomas
focus: Archaeological remote sensing • LiDAR mapping • Maya landscape analysis
bio: Luke S. Auld-Thomas is an archaeologist and Assistant Professor in the Department of Anthropology at Northern Arizona University, specializing in the application of remote sensing technologies to archaeological research. His work focuses primarily on the Maya Lowlands of Central America, where he uses advanced tools such as LiDAR and aerial imaging to investigate ancient urbanism, population patterns, and long-term environmental change. Auld-Thomas’s research explores how past societies shaped their landscapes and how those impacts continue to influence modern ecosystems. By integrating archaeological theory with cutting-edge geospatial technologies, he contributes to a growing field that is transforming how researchers study ancient civilizations. His work highlights the power of remote sensing to reveal hidden structures and settlement systems beneath dense forest canopies, offering new insights into the scale and complexity of pre-Columbian societies
